# data/ — machine-generated record store

**Everything in this directory is generated by the Sakshi pipeline. Do not hand-edit any file here.**

The entire `data/` tree is rebuilt idempotently on every pipeline run. Writes are
atomic (write to a temporary file, validate, then rename into place), so a run that
fails partway leaves the previous good state untouched, and re-running is always safe.
Any manual change will be silently overwritten on the next run — and, worse, would
bypass the sanitize and PII-guard gates that every published record must pass. If a
record is wrong, fix the source or the pipeline, never the file.

## License

The contents of `data/` are published under the **Open Database License (ODbL) v1.0**.
(Project *code* is MIT; the *data* is ODbL. See the repository root for full terms.)

## Files

### `summary.json`
The landing-page payload. Contains only aggregates — never full records:
- `total` — total published cases
- `status_counts` — count per status enum token
- `state_counts` — count per 2-letter state/UT code
- `monthly_trend` — last 24 months of counts
- `top_longest_pending` — top-10 longest-pending cases, carrying **only** `id`,
  `district`, and `days` (no re-identifying detail)
- `generated_at` — ISO-8601 timestamp of the run

**Hard limit: `summary.json` must stay under 50 KB.** CI asserts this. It is the first
and often only thing the frontend loads, and it is cached offline by the service worker.

### `index.json`
The shard manifest so the frontend never has to guess-and-404. Lists every available
`{YYYY}/{STATE}.json` shard with its record count and byte size:

```
{"generated_at": "...", "shards": [
  {"path": "2026/TG.json", "state": "TG", "year": 2026, "records": 0, "bytes": 0}
]}
```

### `{YYYY}/{STATE}.json`
Per-year, per-state arrays of full records, sorted by date descending. `{STATE}` is the
2-letter Indian state/UT code (TG, AP, MH, DL, KA, ...). Record shape is defined by
`schemas/case.schema.json`, which is the single source of truth.

**Shard-split rule:** if a shard would exceed 500 KB it is automatically split into
`{STATE}-p2.json` (then `-p3.json`, ...), and every part is recorded in `index.json`.

## `data/_review/` — quarantine (gitignored, not published)

Low-confidence extractions (`confidence < 0.8`) and ambiguous dedup merges are written
to `data/_review/` for human review. This directory is **gitignored**, is **excluded
from GitHub Pages**, and never feeds the frontend. Nothing leaves quarantine except by
human decision. If the review queue grows past 20 items, the pipeline auto-opens an issue.

## What this store never contains

Victim identity is **never ingested** — not redacted, never written. Dedup is
case-anchored (FIR / CNR / police station / district / date / court), never keyed on any
person's identity. Accused names appear only when present in an official court record.
See the repository root guardrails and `sanitize.py` / `pii_guard.py` for enforcement.
